To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{pmatrix} $$

Let's convert all fractions to have a common denominator of 16 to simplify the calculations.
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix} $$
Let $B = \begin{pmatrix} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{pmatrix}$. Then $A = \frac{1}{16}B$.
The characteristic polynomial is $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det\left(\frac{1}{16}B - \lambda I\right) = \det\left(\frac{1}{16}(B - 16\lambda I)\right)$.
Using the property $\det(kM) = k^n \det(M)$ for an $n \times n$ matrix, here $n=3$:
$$ p(\lambda) = \left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^3 \det(B - 16\lambda I) = \frac{1}{4096} \det(B - 16\lambda I) $$
